codeReview.summary: The backend covers the main batch-generation flow, but regeneration can silently erase a previously completed generated synthetic user when the LLM call fails. That breaks the expected 'regenerate a single user via LLM keeping axis values' behavior because a retry can downgrade a usable user into a permanently failed shell with no profile fields.

issues.description: `regenerateSyntheticUser` patches an existing generated user back to `pending_expansion` and then `claimSyntheticUserForRegeneration` flips it to `expanding`, but `failSyntheticUserExpansion` only records `generationStatus`/`generationError` on failure. If the LLM call fails during regeneration, the document keeps the in-place partially reset state instead of restoring the last completed profile, so a previously usable generated user is left failed and may no longer satisfy callers expecting populated profile fields (`firstPersonBio`, `behaviorRules`, `tensionSeed`). This is a data-loss bug in the retry path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker intentionally deviated from `backend-worker` step 6 because the mission documents a known flaky/slow full-suite `bun run test`. The skill still mandates the full suite, so the procedure does not match mission reality for this repo.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md` step 6 requires `bun run test`; handoff `skillFeedback.deviations` says the worker used scoped validators instead because mission `AGENTS.md` documents the BrowserLeaseDO full-suite issue.

summary: Fail: found a blocking regeneration bug. If regeneration's LLM expansion fails, the existing generated user is left in a failed/half-reset state rather than preserving its previous completed profile. Report written to the requested scrutiny review path.
